consistency.level_and_school: Consistent. Level 7 conjuration aligns with the scope of planar travel.
consistency.casting_time_and_duration: Consistent. Instantaneous result after an action casting time.
consistency.components: Component cost (250 gp, attuned rod) is high, which is appropriate for a 7th-level planar travel spell. The requirement of attunement to a specific plane needs careful tracking.
consistency.class_availability: Appropriate mix of divine (Cleric, Druid) and arcane (Sorcerer, Warlock, Wizard) casters for a high-level reality-altering spell.

quality.description_clarity: The description is comprehensive, clearly separating the willing travel option from the unwilling banishment option.
quality.ambiguity: The GM discretion on the exact arrival point ('on a street, before a gate, or looking at the city from across a sea') is standard for teleportation spells but introduces minor narrative control.
quality.mechanical_depth: High mechanical depth due to two distinct primary functions: mass travel and targeted banishment (which requires a melee spell attack and a saving throw).

willing_transport.mechanism: Linked circle (8 willing creatures + caster). No save required.
willing_transport.target_specificity: Can be general (named city) or specific (teleportation circle sigil sequence).

unwilling_banishment.mechanism: Melee spell attack followed by a Charisma saving throw.
unwilling_banishment.consequence_of_failure: Transported to a random location on the specified plane. The target must find its own way back, making this an effective, albeit not instant-death, removal tool.

mechanics.resource_management: The attuned material component is a high-cost, single-use item until re-attuned or potentially lost, adding a significant economic consideration to repeated use.

analysis.summary: Plane Shift is a well-defined 7th-level spell offering powerful transportation utility for the party or significant removal capability via banishment. The material component cost reinforces its high-tier status. Mechanics are sound and cover both cooperative and hostile casting scenarios.
